landing.tagline: Convert your sheet music to dark mode
landing.description: Reading sheet music on screen at night, or on a dark stage, with the traditional white background is exhausting for your eyes. Dark Score intelligently inverts the colors of your PDFs and images: it removes scan noise, thickens staff lines, and applies the color scheme you prefer.

privacy.badge: Your scores never leave your device
privacy.body: We understand your scores are important to you. That is why this site never copies or sends your files to any external server. All processing happens directly in your browser, with no external connections.

header.localTooltip: Your files are processed entirely in your browser. Nothing is uploaded to any server.

upload.hint: or click to select · PDF, PNG, JPG · max. {{size}} MB · multiple files supported
upload.privacy: Your scores never leave your device
upload.errorFormat: Unsupported format. Please use PDF, PNG or JPG.

controls.label: Adjustments
controls.dilationWarning: High level: notes in dense passages may bleed together.

why.title: Why Dark Score?
why.body: Musicians who practice at night, perform on dark stages, or read from tablets for hours know the problem: white sheet music on a bright screen is tiring for the eyes. Dark Score was born to solve this. It converts your PDFs and images to dark mode in seconds, so you can focus on the music.

nightOwls.title: Night owls
nightOwls.body: Practice late without eye strain. The blue light filter preset is designed for long sessions in low light.

stagePerformers.title: Stage performers
stagePerformers.body: A bright white screen in a dark theater distracts the audience and ruins your night vision. The dark stage preset gives you AMOLED black with high contrast notation.

orchestraPit.title: Orchestra pit musicians
orchestraPit.body: Pit musicians need to read music under dim stand lights. The warm amber preset reduces glare while keeping notation readable.

teachers.title: Teachers and students
teachers.body: Preparing materials for screen-based lessons? Convert handouts to dark mode so students can read comfortably on any device.

custom.title: Custom needs
custom.body: Choose your own background and notation colors, adjust contrast and brightness, and thicken thin lines from old scans. Make your scores look exactly how you want.

privacy.title: Your files stay on your device
privacy.howItWorks: Dark Score processes everything directly in your browser using a technology called Web Workers. This means:
points: Your files are never uploaded to any server
No internet connection is needed after the page loads
No one else can see or access your scores
The source code is open and auditable on GitHub
privacy.why: I built it this way because I understand that sheet music can be copyrighted material, personal arrangements, or unpublished compositions. Your work deserves to stay yours.
